paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And this is the law of the trespass-offering: it is most holy.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: In the place where they kill the burnt-offering shall they kill the trespass-offering; and the blood thereof shall he sprinkle upon the altar round about.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he shall offer of it all the fat thereof: the fat tail, and the fat that covereth the inwards,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the loins, and the caul upon the liver, with the kidneys, shall he take away;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar for an offering made by fire unto Jehovah: it is a trespass-offering.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Every male among the priests shall eat thereof: it shall be eaten in a holy place: it is most holy.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: As is the sin-offering, so is the trespass-offering; there is one law for them: the priest that maketh atonement therewith, he shall have it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the priest that offereth any man`s burnt-offering, even the priest shall have to himself the skin of the burnt-offering which he hath offered.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And every meal-offering that is baken in the oven, and all that is dressed in the frying-pan, and on the baking-pan, shall be the priest`s that offereth it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And every meal-offering, mingled with oil, or dry, shall all the sons of Aaron have, one as well as another.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace-offerings, which one shall offer unto Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: If he offer it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the sacrifice of thanksgiving unleavened cakes mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and cakes mingled with oil, of fine flour soaked.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: With cakes of leavened bread he shall offer his oblation with the sacrifice of his peace-offerings for thanksgiving.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And of it he shall offer one out of each oblation for a heave-offering unto Jehovah; it shall be the priest`s that sprinkleth the blood of the peace-offerings.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace-offerings for thanksgiving shall be eaten on the day of his oblation; he shall not leave any of it until the morning.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But if the sacrifice of his oblation be a vow, or a freewill-offering, it shall be eaten on the day that he offereth his sacrifice; and on the morrow that which remaineth of it shall be eaten:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: but that which remaineth of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burnt with fire.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace-offerings be eaten on the third day, it shall not be accepted, neither shall it be imputed unto him that offereth it: it shall be an abomination, and the soul that eateth of it shall bear his iniquity.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the flesh that toucheth any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burnt with fire. And as for the flesh, every one that is clean shall eat thereof:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: but the soul that eateth of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace-offerings, that pertain unto Jehovah, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when any one shall touch any unclean thing, the uncleanness of man, or an unclean beast, or any unclean abomination, and eat of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace-offerings, which pertain unto Jehovah, that soul shall be cut off from his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Ye shall eat no fat, of ox, or sheep, or goat.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the fat of that which dieth of itself, and the fat of that which is torn of beasts, may be used for any other service; but ye shall in no wise eat of it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto Jehovah, even the soul that eateth it shall be cut off from his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And ye shall eat no manner of blood, whether it be of bird or of beast, in any of your dwellings.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Whosoever it be that eateth any blood, that soul shall be cut off from his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, He that offereth the sacrifice of his peace-offerings unto Jehovah shall bring his oblation unto Jehovah out of the sacrifice of his peace-offerings:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: his own hands shall bring the offerings of Jehovah made by fire; the fat with the breast shall he bring, that the breast may be waved for a wave-offering before Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar; but the breast shall be Aaron`s and his sons`.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the right thigh shall ye give unto the priest for a heave-offering out of the sacrifices of your peace-offerings.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: He among the sons of Aaron that offereth the blood of the peace-offerings, and the fat, shall have the right thigh for a portion.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For the wave-breast and the heave-thigh have I taken of the children of Israel out of the sacrifices of their peace-offerings, and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons as [their] portion for ever from the children of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: This is the anointing-portion of Aaron, and the anointing-portion of his sons, out of the offerings of Jehovah made by fire, in the day when he presented them to minister unto Jehovah in the priest`s office;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: which Jehovah commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them. It is [their] portion for ever throughout their generations.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: This is the law of the burnt-offering, of the meal-offering, and of the sin-offering, and of the trespass-offering, and of the consecration, and of the sacrifice of peace-offerings;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: which Jehovah commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations unto Jehovah, in the wilderness of Sinai.
